title: Lower Cirrus-CI CPU + Re-add verify step
body: Don't waste GCE VM resources for 30-min of testing, when verify would fail after 30-seconds.

comments.body: ***Note***: 38-seconds to cirrus-ci failure because of missing commit-signature (DCO).  Openshift validate took more minutes.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Confirmed: Cirrus spent 0:37 seconds scheduling, 0:28 in ``setup_environment.sh``, 0:40 in ``verify_source.sh`` (only doing ``make validate``, no lint).  According to origin-ci, ci/prow/validate took 6:35.  So we should at least to some simple idiot-checking before committing to 35-min of GCE time.

For the future, a more robust check could be to have cirrus poll the PR status and wait (with a timeout) for certain things to pass (like ``ci/prow/validate``).  If that happened from inside a container, using their community-cluster, then we don't spend any GCE CPU time needlessly.
